title: Vulnerability Insight and Action (VIA)
content: Vulnerability Insight and Action's mission is to reduce human and animal suffering and vulnerability to disasters and crises.
snippet: Vulnerability Insight and Action's mission is to reduce human and animal suffering and vulnerability to disasters and crises.

title: Massive Australian bushfire impacts: it's our fault
content: This report examines how large, severe bushfires in South East Australia are causing widespread environmental, social, and economic damage, worsened by inadequate fire management and increasing fire intensity.

title: The emerging danger of post-hurricane heat waves
content: With global warming making people increasingly dependent on air conditioning, power failures from hurricanes followed by heat waves are creating increasingly hazardous risks to health.
snippet: With global warming making people increasingly dependent on air conditioning, power failures from hurricanes followed by heat waves are creating increasingly hazardous risks to health.

title: Synergistic integration of flood inundation modeling methods
content: Recent flood modeling advances are trending into silos that compete rather than complement each other, hampering the opportunity for transformative progress toward protecting lives and communities.
snippet: Recent flood modeling advances are trending into silos that compete rather than complement each other, hampering the opportunity for transformative progress toward protecting lives and communities.

title: Governments must fix ‘faulty radar’ in economic climate models as storm approaches, scientists warn
content: New report shows how closer alignment between scientific estimates and economic modeling of physical risks is possible, as world moves towards 2°C.
snippet: New report shows how closer alignment between scientific estimates and economic modeling of physical risks is possible, as world moves towards 2°C.

title: Addressing vulnerability to urban heat stress through community engagement: a systematic scoping review

title: Argentine wildfire AI startup raises $2.7M after building a detection system that beats NASA’s alerts by 35 minutes
content: The company builds an AI-powered wildfire detection platform that integrates satellite imagery, tower cameras, fire propagation modelling, and real-time alerts, and says its system detects fires on average 35 minutes ahead of NASA’s FIRMS service.

title: Sustainable disaster mitigation through integrated nature and engineering solutions: How mangroves save lives — Hiroshi Takagi

title: Where do evacuees go? Understanding evacuation destinations in Georgia during Hurricane Milton
content: This study proposes a methodological framework to analyze spatial and temporal patterns of Florida residents' evacuation to Georgia during Hurricane Milton, focusing on changes in Points-of-Interest (POIs) visitor share within Georgia census tracts.

title: Risk governance or risky governance? Delhi’s urban utility governance landscape and heatwaves

title: Trees4heatresilience: evaluating tree canopy coverage to reduce air conditioning demand in Honduran urban neighborhoods

title: ‘Radar holes’ leave one in five Americans vulnerable to surprise tornadoes
content: If a tornado were about to hit your house, what would you do? Of course, your answer depends on your physical capabilities and your proximity to a basement or other safe space.
snippet: If a tornado were about to hit your house, what would you do? Of course, your answer depends on your physical capabilities and your proximity to a basement or other safe space.

title: Post‑flood recovery in Europe requires better preparation
content: Flooding caused by extreme and prolonged rainfall is occurring more frequently across Europe. The way countries recover afterwards varies widely. These differences offer valuable lessons.
snippet: Flooding caused by extreme and prolonged rainfall is occurring more frequently across Europe. The way countries recover afterwards varies widely. These differences offer valuable lessons.

title: Warming intensifies rainfall in North Atlantic storms
content: Rapid ocean warming is likely to make tropical cyclone rainfall more intense and longer lasting, increasing flood risks in parts of the North Atlantic region.
snippet: Rapid ocean warming is likely to make tropical cyclone rainfall more intense and longer lasting, increasing flood risks in parts of the North Atlantic region.

title: Science of seismic proportions
content: Arizona’s deserts might not be situated on a tectonic plate boundary where geographical activity can cause earthquakes, but that doesn’t make the state immune from the disturbance of tremors.
